programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: One man transfixed television viewers during snookers golden age - his name was Alex `Hurricane Higgins. This poignant documentary charts the life of snookers first genius, from his early days growing up in Belfast to his climb to the top of the sport as two-time world champion. Contributors include Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Dennis Taylor, Steve Davis, and members of the Higgins family.

programme.title: Gold Run
programme.desc: In 1940, the Germans invaded Norway with clear goals in mind: the king, the government, to control the coast, the iron ore and the Norwegian gold reserves. At the same time, a group of resistance fighters was given a most important and secret mission: save Norways gold.

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Escapologist Harry Houdini was one of the icons of the early 20th century. Restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists recall his extraordinary impact on audiences and reveal the secrets behind some of his most amazing escapes.

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Professor Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary and revolutionary periods in British history: the Industrial Revolution. He explains the unique economic, social and political conditions that by the 19th century, led to Britain becoming the richest, most powerful nation on the planet. It was a time that transformed the way people think, work and play forever.

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow travels to Beaumaris Castle on the island of Anglesey, north Wales, where jewellery expert Joanna Hardy is dazzled by a rare ruby and diamond collection worn by a family for generations of weddings. Lisa Lloyd is fascinated by a wooden figurine hidden in a secret cupboard and identifies it as a ceremonial African puppet. Ben Rogers Jones sees a collection of photographs and ephemera relating to one of Waless most important historical figures, Prime Minister David Lloyd George.

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Scams and Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Its Christmas at Button House and Mike and Alison are heading to Mikes parents to celebrate with family. As Mike piles up the Tupperware with what he insists are only side dishes, despite one clearly being an entire turkey, Kitty expresses her disappointment at not spending Christmas day with Alison. Alison promises that theyll be back by the evening and whats more, reveals to the ghosts that there is a Christmas surprise for each of them in the library.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Its coming up to Christmas, and Mikes mum is staying. Betty quickly overstays her welcome, but Alison and Mike dont quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isnt your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Mirandas joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides its time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so hell see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than hed originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Kens house of all his junk or as Ken calls it, his memories. As Kens suspicions over Richards true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob and Richards former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard offers to take Ken to the local golf club, unaware that hes about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than hed originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists in order to secure Ken a doctors appointment and is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and endeavours to track down Kens oldest friend, who Ken lost touch with years ago, in order to invite him to Kens surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jeans ashes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny is reeling after being chatted up by Louises Dad. He decides to become more macho, but his plan backfires with shocking consequences.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is reeling that Gaz tried to take Corinthian and decides to increase security in the home, while Jonny gets a job as a farmer. programme.title: Doctor Who

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isnt your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Mirandas joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides its time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so hell see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.desc: Small is pretending to be a detective as something has eaten all the salad in the garden. Wiggly Woo the worm comes to visit, Small flies off on her spoon to find out how rhubarb is harvested, and the two chefs cook a delicious worm crumble for their wiggly guest.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to fly their paper planes. On the way they discover a secret squirrel den, sit on a dragonfly, race like boats and even walk like dinosaurs.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum is determined to perform Great-Granny Plums song at the Balamory ceilidh all by himself, but he soon realises it isnt easy being a one-man band.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggees nephew Dudley comes to stay. He is one cute puppy, but he looks like he could cause chaos in the clubhouse. And whats that terrible smell?! The Squirrels come up with lots of imaginative ways to entertain him but thereis only one character that can stop him crying. Who do you think it could be?

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When hungry gulper eels as well as undersea tremors threaten Inkling and Kwazii, Dashi uses the new, multi-purpose Octo Max Suit to rescue them.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit gets a mosquito bite on picture day, and he doesnt want a photo. However, Vida has an idea.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters visit St Basils Cathedral, but birthday boy Grandmaster Glitch has glitched it.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Its raining, so Rafi and Ruby are playing indoor football. But when they accidentally break a plate, Rafi wishes for it to be fixed but warns them not to play again.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes and Toad see a beautiful sunrise and decide to write a song about it together, but Stripes wants the song to be rocky, while Toad wants it to be slower. When they fall out and go off to write their own songs separately, Yukee tries to help them become friends again and create one song together.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Grapefruit dedicating the song All My Friends to the close pals who go with her everywhere.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends are on a pirate adventure, searching for buried treasure. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is struggling to decide which story to read to Tumble Ted.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Suuvi, Margot, Jasper, Sammie, Teddy and Tehlani, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too. The `Whats in the Box? game reveals some wooden eggs, a toy chicken and a soft basket, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by things that might be found on the farm.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou are upset with each other but cant remember why.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: The Piggies build a castle so that Rebecca can be a knight and start an epic quest. But will everything go smoothly with Wilf as the dragon?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join sisters Eden and Ava as they become superheroes! First they create their characters Nature Bunny Girl and H-Winter Girl - not forgetting new personas for Mum and Dad too!

programme.title: Stan Can
programme.desc: Stanley helps the kids play their big football match in all weather conditions, escalating into his latest creation, a high tech stadium!

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: When they land on the planet of Fairy Tales, the Fairy Tales meet two alien children. They immediately want to play with their new friends, but they are so sleepy that they can hardly keep their eyes open.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Waffles After School Club
programme.desc: When a smelly, dirty Waffle runs away at the mention of baths, everyone must find and clean him in time for him to lead a marching band sing along for their head teacher, Mr Nolan.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Spring is the time of year when lots of different plants grow. Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one thats just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Talking Tom Heroes: Suddenly Super
programme.desc: Hank loses a pile of his idea orbs, and they cause trouble all around the city.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers learn how butterfly feeders can provide a source of energy for butterflies. They spend time designing their own using overripe fruit to help the butterflies in the garden.

programme.title: Andys Dino Island
programme.desc: When Jens drone goes missing on Dino Island, Andy heads out on an epic adventure to track it down. He discovers one of the islands inhabitants, a giraffe-sized pterosaur called Hatzegopteryx, is using the drone for a very special purpose. Along the way, he meets a herd of stampeding triceratops.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Peas parents try to teach her to not lose her temper so she can beat Supertato.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Click Clack becomes a trainee kite pilot and learns how to navigate a kite as Aimee and Mia perform Mary Poppins `Lets Go Fly a Kite in British Sign Language, accompanied by animations.

programme.title: Nick Copes Popcast
programme.desc: The caravan is visited by a little owl learning to fly. The owl and Alva, Nicks granddaughter, give Nick the perfect idea for todays song.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Kipp doesnt know what made him giddy, its up to Vida to solve the dizzy mystery.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard race against time to retrieve sun googles so they can watch a cosmic triple eclipse.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafis zip line wish shows playgrounds are magic when everyone gets a turn.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo are playing The Quiet Game and wont use words to tell Dad which toy to buy. At his wits end, Dad recruits new store clerk Alfie to help interpret the miming.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Its Time to Make Up a Story
programme.desc: When JoJo cant find her favourite Captain Chloe storybook, Gran Gran suggests they make up their own story. As they create their pirate quest, JoJo learns that when you make up your own story, you get to decide what happens next.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady takes to the skies in a knitted hot air balloon to race against an old sporting friend.

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola wants to make Charlies birthday party the best party anyone could ever have. The problem is that Lolas idea of a good party isnt anything like Charlies, and she has to undo a lot of what shes done.

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana, Mr Onion, Little Nana and their friends discover a paper star on the ceiling of the little toy house. In Storyland, they find a sky full of twinkly stars to wonder about.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa explore all different types of connections on Planet BeddyByes.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Travelling in the Pinky Ponk, the Tombliboos are drinking their Pinky Ponk juice. Several times they just avoid spilling their juice by being careful but finally it splats everywhere.

programme.sub-title: Gregory Porter - Singing in the Rain
programme.desc: Stories for bedtime.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles is suspected as an accomplice to murder when his ex-wife Maris uses his crossbow to kill her husband. Daphne is upset when Maris continually calls Niles from jail, and Frasier accidentally recommends that his brother be executed.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Liliths friend unknowingly sets her up on a blind date with Frasier, and Niles and Daphne urge Martin to get rid of his gun.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer sells his soul to the devil for a doughnut, no one believes Barts story about the goblin intent on sabotaging the school bus, and Mr Burns invites the Simpsons over for a bite at his spooky castle.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons holiday at Niagara Falls, where Marge must rescue Lisa when a mix-up sees her mistakenly granted asylum in Canada. Elsewhere, Homer takes Maggie to a `Daddy and Me class.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homers work cuts childrens health care benefits, Marge turns to buying healing crystals as a cheaper solution for Barts ADD. Bart begins to succeed in school, prompting Marge to open her own crystals empire.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB battle begins at Michael and Danielles Fountain Cottage in Bellingham, Northumberland. The hosts are hoping their award-winning cafe will give them the edge, but its a trip to a local brewery that really gets the guests in high spirits.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit two takes the BandBers to The Shuckburgh Arms in Stoke Doyle, Northamptonshire. Everyone goes barmy for their converted barns, but one guest isnt buzzing about a broken fridge disturbing his slumber.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Great Yarmouth for the weeks third visit, at Stu and Lous Beachside Holidays. No ones arguing with the stunning sea views and the location right on the beach, but the hosts take some flack for the lack of a welcome pack.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final visit of the week, and the BandBers are down the rabbit hole at the Alice in Wonderland-themed Hatters Hotel in Skegness. Dee and Gary reckon their unique BandB experience will have their guests grinning like Cheshire cats.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Edinburgh locals perform the one song that tells their story - from the tune a 22-year-old played at his mums funeral to the Zach Bryan track that helped a former soldiers PTSD.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.desc: Nilesh and Raakhee moved into their four-bed home in Maidenhead two years ago. Despite Nileshs hard work decorating throughout, hes ready to list it. But Raakhee believes that with a clever plan they could already be living in their forever home.

programme.desc: Liliths friend unknowingly sets her up on a blind date with Frasier, and Niles and Daphne urge Martin to get rid of his gun.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer sells his soul to the devil for a doughnut, no one believes Barts story about the goblin intent on sabotaging the school bus, and Mr Burns invites the Simpsons over for a bite at his spooky castle.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons holiday at Niagara Falls, where Marge must rescue Lisa when a mix-up sees her mistakenly granted asylum in Canada. Elsewhere, Homer takes Maggie to a `Daddy and Me class.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homers work cuts childrens health care benefits, Marge turns to buying healing crystals as a cheaper solution for Barts ADD. Bart begins to succeed in school, prompting Marge to open her own crystals empire.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB battle begins at Michael and Danielles Fountain Cottage in Bellingham, Northumberland. The hosts are hoping their award-winning cafe will give them the edge, but its a trip to a local brewery that really gets the guests in high spirits.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Visit two takes the BandBers to The Shuckburgh Arms in Stoke Doyle, Northamptonshire. Everyone goes barmy for their converted barns, but one guest isnt buzzing about a broken fridge disturbing his slumber.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Great Yarmouth for the weeks third visit, at Stu and Lous Beachside Holidays. No ones arguing with the stunning sea views and the location right on the beach, but the hosts take some flack for the lack of a welcome pack.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final visit of the week, and the BandBers are down the rabbit hole at the Alice in Wonderland-themed Hatters Hotel in Skegness. Dee and Gary reckon their unique BandB experience will have their guests grinning like Cheshire cats.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Great Yarmouth for the weeks third visit, at Stu and Lous Beachside Holidays. No ones arguing with the stunning sea views and the location right on the beach, but the hosts take some flack for the lack of a welcome pack.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowes helping Claire from Bedfordshire find a holiday home in the heart of Mallorcas capital, Palma. Claire has a budget of £190,000 and Craig has five great properties to show her.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Edinburgh locals perform the one song that tells their story - from the tune a 22-year-old played at his mums funeral to the Zach Bryan track that helped a former soldiers PTSD.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: We revisit Shane, who dreamt of a new life on the north Kent coast, and Indu and Jason, who wanted a family home with potential in Guildford. Did they get what they wished for?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Nilesh and Raakhee moved into their four-bed home in Maidenhead two years ago. Despite Nileshs hard work decorating throughout, hes ready to list it. But Raakhee believes that with a clever plan they could already be living in their forever home.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: Secret Africa: Into The Wild
programme.desc: Explorer Lucy Shepherd embarks on an epic journey across Tanzania with the nomadic Indigenous Hadza and Akie tribesmen. With no crew and no safety net, survival over the next six weeks depends on working with the wild rather than against it.

programme.title: Worlds Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: The global search for faraway places continues with a majestic fortress-style hotel in the United Arab Emirates, a futuristic fantasy in Dorset, and a yellow submarine in Ireland.

programme.title: Living
programme.desc: A veteran civil servant receives a medical diagnosis that inspires him to move to the south coast and cram some fun into his remaining days. He meets a sunny young female colleague who seems to have the pep that had previously escaped him.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Theyll be travelling from their home in Essex all the way to the Amalfi Coast in Italy, but the first stop on their road trip is the Netherlands, where they try out one of the countrys oldest sports, canal jumping. The action continues when the pair don lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is literally pushed to the edge as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel, and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The next stop on Bradley and Barney Walshs European road trip is Switzerland. They take in the breath-taking views with a ride on a mountain rollercoaster, before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodeling. The following day, Barney arranges a unique way for them to enjoy some local cheese, and then drive to the Contra Dam, where Barney reveals they are about to attempt one of James Bonds most iconic stunts, a petrifying 220 metre-high bungee jump.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The father and son have reached Slovenia, a country that loves its sport. Brads passion for bikes gives Barney an excuse to take his dad motocross racing. After hearing that one in 200 people in Slovenia is a beekeeper, they make a stop to meet one.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the penultimate leg of their journey Bradley and Barney head to Italy, starting by the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they will be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. Soon after, Bradley takes a dip in the lake when he tries paddleboard yoga. The next day the guys travel to the River Po for a boat trip like no other, as Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Barney and Bradley Walsh embark on the final leg of their European road trip, as they continue to explore Italy. Having reached Rome, they take a trip back in time to battle it out as gladiators. They continue south and make a stop to soak up the views from a sky high zipwire. As the boys reach their final stop on the Amalfi Coast, they get the chance to swim in the stunning sea, but theyll have to cliff dive in first.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The boys are back! Bradley and Barney Walsh embark on a 3,000-mile journey through Europe, starting off in the sunny south of Croatia and ending up in the ice-cold Arctic Circle in Scandinavia. Theres no easing his dad in on this trip for Barney. First up, they swing off the roof of Splits iconic football stadium, before diving headfirst into the crystal clear waters of the Adriatic Sea. The action continues when the pair go head to head on aqua bikes alongside a former world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney leave Croatia behind and say hello to Hungary, where the boys take to the open waters of Lake Balaton for a spot of high-speed catamaran sailing before warming up with some world-famous goulash. The pair finish their time in Budapest, where Barney and Bradley meet a world-famous escapologist.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney continue their European adventure in Poland. The boys take in a spot of sightseeing in Krakow, before they don wetsuits and head to a freezing cold lake to try out water blobbing. Next up, they come face to face with mans best friend as Brad gets chased by an attack dog. All before Barney treats his dad to an exclusive performance with the Polish National Radio Symphony Orchestra in honour of Polands most famous composer, Chopin.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney arrive in Denmark for the Scandinavian leg of their tour. Bradleys fear of heights is pushed to the limit with a visit to an offshore wind turbine, and the boys test both of their boundaries with a visit to the worlds tallest indoor rope course.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Conspiracies and Cover-Ups
programme.desc: Utilising the resources of the New York Post, this is an in-depth look into the biggest government conspiracies and coverups.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Countdown of NASAs top six the deadliest stars - vampire stars that drain their neighbours, supernovas and white dwarfs that destroy planets.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Despite Plutos appearance as a dark and frozen world, scientists are investigating whether there might be warm liquid water beneath the surface.

programme.desc: Memphis is in the Las Vegas desert for a small tire shootout; when Precious and Tricia lock in races with a few West Coast heavy hitters, MSO puts more than $25,000 on the line in side bets.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike tracks down a rare 91 Alfa Romeo 164L. He loves the look, but Ant has his work cut out for him. The car has perfect bodywork, but it has a leaking engine.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds what he thinks is the finest-handling front wheel drive car, the Lotus Elan M100. If Ant can revive the engine and suspension, will it make a profit?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a 2002 Chevy Corvette Z06 that can do zero to 60 in under four seconds. Ant is less than enthused when he realises the scale of the work needed.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The Golf GTi was a symbol of the 80s and gave birth to the term "hot hatch"; Mike finds a prime example of a Rabbit which he believes can be transformed; the market is crying out for cars like this, but Ant has a lot of work to do.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The Dodge SRT 10 truck has an 8.3 litre V10 Viper engine, and Mike finds one at a price he cant resist. Once Ant is sold on the plan, they dive in, working to make this vehicle powerful enough to dominate on a NASCAR track.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: From one of the fastest trucks on earth, to one of the ugliest cars ever made, Mike and Ant have tackled a wide range of motors. The guys get a chance to share some sweet bonus footage, hilarious outtakes, and more fascinating processes.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys rally at Rikas Roadhouse, a legendary homestead established by one of Alaskas toughest female pioneers; gathering inspiration for the summer ahead, they reflect on past rescues and the importance of a well-developed, off-grid mindset.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys put their ingenuity to the test on a homestead in Hawaii that was destroyed by lava. They need to find a way of protecting it from future eruptions.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: A young homesteader and her military boyfriend face a ticking clock in the east Georgia woods. The Raneys must solve the unsanitary conditions, poor soil and crumbling infrastructure before the next big rains hit the homestead.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.sub-title: Raney Returns: Big Sky, Bigger Problems

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Carl and his German wife Anja serve up a meal inspired by Anjas homeland, while Leroy and Hayley cook Caribbean. However, Sangeeta and Sreekanth try to dance their way to the prize.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bedfordshire, Tom and Naomi prepare posh nosh, Victoria and Elliot serve a controversial goat curry and Neil and Charlie plan to impress their guests with mystery and magic.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Lee has just three weeks and £12,000 to pull off the perfect day for his bride-to-be Ellie. Lee wants a huge wedding that celebrates his dwarfism, and shows off his sense of humour. However, all Ellie wants is a low key, elegant and intimate wedding.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: All Hannah wants is a luxurious and relaxing wedding day where she looks a million dollars, but Tommy has a big romantic plan up his sleeve for his ex-water-skiing champ bride.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Petrified Bride
programme.desc: The skys the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai as he plans on having a wedding in the sky, neglecting the fact that his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Party animal Harry has three weeks and £12,000 to pull off a day to impress his perfectionist bride-to-be Rosa, but while she dreams of a stylish, vintage English wedding thats all about romance and tradition. Harry, however, would like a fun full-scale Bollywood-inspired extravaganza.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Fashion Show
programme.desc: Jenna dreams of a traditional and timeless wedding that will never go out of style, James wants a cutting-edge, catwalk fashion show ceremony full of the latest trends.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmanns department stores, goes undercover posing as an aspiring entrepreneur in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to start up his own firm.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jane Grote Abell, chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the companys founder, goes undercover posing as a contestant on a reality TV show who is trying to win money to open up her own restaurant.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay goes back to three restaurants - Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl - to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The opening episode of a new series sees Gordon in Plainfield, New Jersey, trying to save a floundering soul food restaurant called Blackberrys. He comes up against the indomitable Shelley, an owner who runs her establishment with an iron fist, and doesnt want to hear what Gordon has to say. Gordon must find a way to convince her to change her ways if the restaurant is going to stand a chance.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leones from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Its only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his sons inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniels inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedically contorted Countdown mash-up as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Rob Brydon and Joe Wilkinson, with Huge Davies in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Bewilderness
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Bill Bailey performs live at the Grand Theatre in Swansea and delivers hilariously unfocused and scattered comedy styles, as well as his enviable musical skills.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Its July 12th and the Quinn family want to get out of Derry before the Orange Parades start. Aunt Sarahs tarot cards predict a disaster for this trip - and she might be right.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin is over the moon when she becomes editor of the school magazine. Orlas obsessed by step aerobics and Da Gery is in trouble with Ma Mary, and theres romance for Aunt Sarah.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Stand-up comic and presenter Jimmy Carr is at the helm as comedians Sean Lock and Jon Richardson head up the teams for Christmas capers. Guests featuring in the teams for some yuletide fun include Sarah Millican, Stephen Mangan and Joe Wilkinson. With lots of Christmas-themed questions and a sprinkling of tinsel.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by Jack Dee, Claudia Winkleman, David ODoherty and Josh Widdicombe as they thrash out the hot topics of the week by examining surveys, opinion polls and statistics.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedically contorted Countdown mash-up as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Rob Brydon and Joe Wilkinson, with Huge Davies in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in a Circus Tent
programme.desc: Groom Andrew plans a fun-packed big top wedding to transport his fiancee Jenni away from their troubled lives.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Steve recently turned 40 and wants to transport his bride-to-be Nicki back to the early nineties with a Guns N Roses-inspired wedding. The only problem is, back in the day Nicki only pretended to like the rock scene to get Stephens attention.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One on a Cruise Ship
programme.desc: Childrens entertainer Mark wants to stage a theatrical wedding aboard a cruise ship for his gorgeous bride-to-be Kelly, who wants a traditional, fairy tale affair. But it could be curtains when his bride discovers his outrageous plans.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Anthony Wedo, president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover posing as a construction worker taking part in a reality TV competition to win enough money to start up his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Amit Kleinberger, CEO and co-owner of frozen yogurt chain Menchies, goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund and develop a new business.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, Callum and Alison put together a fun-filled menu, despite Callums not-so-cheery nature. And theres an outburst over the Essex dialect during dessert at Mike and Carols.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, partners Mike and Heather hope to impress their guests with a menu of Mediterranean-inspired dishes. Day two sees Dagenham duo Clare and Dan serve up a menu of locally sourced dishes. Finally, its the turn of Jess and Salv to host.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Midlands, image-conscious couple Sarah and Jack kick off the competition with a clean-living menu. Day two sees married couple Liz and Martin cook an Italian feast inspired by recipes from Lizs Italian mother.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigis was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigis beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Parks Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasnt taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesnt slow down hes in danger of collapse.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Oakhurst, New Jersey, to help Mike Farber, the overwhelmed owner of Mike and Nellies restaurant, who lost his way when Nellie, his father and business partner, died. Opened in 1996, the restaurant was a lifelong dream for the father and son. Alone at the helm, and still mourning the loss of his father, Mike has taken his eye off the ball and the restaurant has gone to pot.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Austin, Texas, home to struggling restaurant El Greco, a business in danger of imminent collapse. El Greco is owned by mother and son Athina and Jake, and claims to be an authentic Greek restaurant. Much to Gordons disgust, the duo have secretly recruited `Chef Mike the microwave to help them out on a daily basis.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Alex Horne treats the contestants to a song, but not everyone appreciates it. A grunting Kumail Nanjiani embraces Amy Gledhill and Joel Dommett while they quiz him about a horse.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Cats Does Countdown does Christmas. Joe Lycett and Danny Dyer play Jon Richardson and Roisin Conaty. With Mr Swallow and Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: In his 1996 debut live stand-up show, Bill Bailey demonstrates the influence that Cockney music has had on the great classical composers, and warns of the perils of a world ruled by insects, while playing the second-biggest gong in Britain.

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin and the gang are excited about an outdoor pursuits peace initiative for Protestant and Catholic school kids. But peace is the last thing on Michelles mind with the Protestant lads there.

programme.desc: Four friends find themselves in over their heads when one of them gets kidnapped by a notorious drug lord in Thailand. As they fight to stay alive and protect one another, they employ a unique set of skills in a heart-pumping battle for survival.

programme.title: Annie
programme.desc: Ever since her parents left her as a baby, little Annie has led a hard-knock life with her mean foster-mother, Miss Hannigan. However, that all changes when hard-nosed billionaire and mayor candidate Will Stacks takes her in on the recommendation of his advisers. Stacks believes that hes Annies guardian angel, but the plucky youngsters confidence and sunny outlook may mean that Annie will save Will instead.

programme.title: Rosaline
programme.desc: A comedic retelling of Shakespeares "Romeo and Juliet," told from the point of view of Romeos jilted ex, Rosaline, the woman Romeo first claims to love before he falls for Juliet.

programme.desc: On June 4, 1942, the Japanese navy plans a strike against American ships in the Pacific. For the next three days, the U.S. Navy and a squad of brave fighter pilots engage the enemy in one of the most important and decisive battles of World War II.

programme.desc: Guy has Italian family recipes in New York, visits a Japanese grill turned diner in California, meets fans for burgers in Wisconsin.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a crepe joint in Colorado, a Jewish deli in New York, that serves kreplach and has lamb cheese steak in Washington.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a Cajun joint for smoked tasso, a Napoletana pizza place in San Antonio, Texas, and a Greek diner in Denver, Colorado.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Big Breakfast Buffet
programme.desc: Ina entertains her serious foodie friends Devon Fredericks and Eli Zabar with a breakfast bash. The pressure is on as Ina creates a buffet of potato pancetta frittata, overnight Belgian waffles and a fresh fruit platter.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its a weekend of photos, fun and style as Inas friend, photographer Miguel comes to visit. For his arrival, she makes spicy hermit bars, followed by the next days lunch of courgette and goat cheese tart, and tomato carpaccio.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina shares her kitchen tips and professional strategies for chicken. Recipes include roasted chicken with radishes, chicken and spinach Waldorf salad, and truffled chicken liver mousse.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like A Pro: Weeknight Dinners
programme.desc: Inas got weeknight dinners covered, pro-style! Recipes include roasted salmon tacos, cider roasted pork tenderloin and five-minute lemon capellini.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Fearless Food
programme.desc: Ina tackles daunting dishes with easy techniques, from a perfectly risen souffle à lorange to overcoming pastry fears with her totally professional-looking Annas tomato tart. Plus, a foolproof caramel sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its eggs all the way in an incredible master class taught by Ina. Recipes include Caesar salad with blue cheese and bacon, a pavlova fruit tart and eggs Benedict with an easy hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes root vegetables to another level, makes scrumptious sticky BBQ chicken, rattles out oozy cheesy enchiladas and turns things upside down with a pineapple cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.55 per portion. Jamie cooks up a glorious mothership roast side of salmon with all the trimmings. Then, then turns his leftover salmon into an incredible salmon filo pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: All the recipes in this episode cost under £1.90 per portion. Jamie cooks up a `mothership roast chicken with all the trimmings and turns the leftovers into chicken stew with dumplings.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Theyre enjoying the luxury trip of a lifetime aboard The Royal Scotsman. James visits the kitchen aboard the train, where he cooks up a traditional duck a lorange. Nick visits a special bakery housed in an old prisoner of war camp and James uses the bread to make a tasty ham and cheese toastie in the grounds of historic Scone Palace. The day ends back aboard the Royal Scotsman, where James and Nick dine in style in the beautiful dining room.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a trip to the stunning Cairngorms National Park, where hes joined by chef pal Nick Nairn. Amongst their adventures, they visit some friendly reindeer and brave the rapids on the River Spey, where they go whitewater rafting. James takes a quad bike through the ancient pine forests, where he meets up with a farmer and his special breed of Highland cattle, which then inspires him to cook a tasty Highland beef carpaccio.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are back in Scotland, but this time, theyre in the beautiful region of Dumfries and Galloway. Its another place close to Dave and Sis hearts, a rural paradise with a glorious coastline that stretches for more than 200 miles.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore Lancashire. For Dave, the trip is an emotional homecoming, as he was born in the county. They start their trip in the 120 square miles of glorious beauty thats Morecambe Bay.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: For their next adventure in the west of Britain, the Bikers are exploring Merseyside and the Wirral, a place that holds a lot of childhood memories for both Dave and Si.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: The Bikers continue their journey by heading to north west Wales, a remote but beautiful part of the UK and home to lush, green pastures, towering peaks, breathtaking valleys and 250 miles of stunning coastline.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less

programme.desc: Guy has a bacon blue cheeseburger in Sacramento, a meatball sandwich in Indianapolis, and turtle soup in Pennsylvania.

programme.desc: Guy visits a dog-friendly joint in Indianapolis, an old jambalaya spot in New Orleans, and a wood fired pizza place in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy tries homemade Italian in East Boston, stuffed burgers in Minnesota, and a great little diner in New Jersey.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia for fried empanadas, a spot in Washington for stuffed pork chops, and a seafood joint in Maine.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. In Kentucky Guy visits a 1951 burger drive-in, a diner in Florida for meatloaf and a 60-year-old breakfast spot in Minnesota.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webbs food frenzy takes him to New Yorks Hudson Valley for a classic autumn, orchard-to-table treat, an Italian dessert for breakfast and wings so hot they have him seeing ghosts in the 4.5-pound Atomic Ghost Wings Challenge.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webbs palate-expanding pilgrimage takes him to Orlando for a hunger-slaying sandwich, a famous footlong and an 8.5 pound team challenge so big that he calls in backup from hometown hero and heartthrob Joey Fatone.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where hes joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James visits a place hes always wanted to see - the beautiful Shetland Islands - and they definitely do not disappoint. James gets to hire a vintage car to explore the area, where he learns about traditional fish salting and cooks a tasty vegetable curry at a local social enterprise project.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Custard Week and the bakers put their twist on floating islands in the Signature, tackle a summer staple, and make custard the star and basis of a showstopping set gateau.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Pastry Week, and the bakers are tasked with making vol-au-vents in the Signature and a delicious savoury snack in the Technical, before creating a showstopping 3D pie-scape depicting their favourite childhood story.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the semi-final and the perfect time to test the remaining bakers abilities in patisserie. There are mini charlottes in the Signature that demand precision, a vertical challenge in the Technical, and finally an opulent Swedish showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its the final, and the last remaining bakers are tasked with making a perfect picnic for the Signature, and for the Technical, a summer classic. And in the final Showstopper, they must create a baked celebration of our planet.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs from all 8 regions must now battle it out for the honour of a spot on 2018s `Feast To Say Thank You banquet menu, celebrating 70 years of the NHS, one of Britains most prized institutions.Each day the regional champions will be cooking one of their courses again for the discerning panel of judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but after some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the starter round, everyone is desperate to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. Once again the chefs present their innovative, witty or heart warming creations, with lobster, mackerel, John Dory, turbot and hake among the dishes being scrutinized.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. Both chefs and judges alike are amazed at the high standard of cooking and an incredible number of 10s are dished out as the chefs serve up venison, rabbit, duck, beef, lamb and pork for scrutiny.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything theyve got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that theyll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomews Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emilys David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britains food heroes and shes using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs need to impress the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate. They are joined by one half of the synthpop duo The Communards, Reverend Richard Coles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom `Westy Westerland is a former National Chef of Wales and head chef of the Brasserie at Lucknam Park in Wiltshire. Cindy Challoner is the former head chef at prestigious teaching restaurant `The Classroom in Cardiff. They face Andy Sheriden formerly of Sosban restaurant in Llanelli, returning to the competition for his second attempt at making it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding puree, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westys beef dish `The Cow Shed is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding puree, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westys beef dish `The Cow Shed is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Wales must cook their four course menus again. They must take on board their veterans advice as they serve their tributes to Britains music success.

When another two deaths occur, both mirroring murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, Lewis and Hathaway realise that something twisted is afoot.

programme.title: Lewis

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: When Gwenda Halliday moves in to the home of her dreams, she begins to see ghostly apparitions. Then it is discovered that Gwenda grew up in the house and the mystery becomes more sinister when she and Miss Marple realise that an unsolved murder was committed there.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Science fellow George Jeffers threatens to go public with what he believes is a potentially lethal software glitch in a new air traffic control system. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of Little Worthy is shattered by a bizarre murder when a man is found stabbed and tied down like Gulliver in Lilliput in the sleepy hamlets model village. programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first day of this new competition is at the Blackthorn Farm BandB on Anglesey, Wales, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to be crowned best value BandB of the week. On arrival, Dan and Rachel Lonergan take in the view, and Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor are feeling the vibes for their morning meditation.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Graham and Barbara Yellow pride themselves on offering basic facilities for great value for money at the White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire. Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch find some hairs out of place in their room, while spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from being in room number four. Over dinner, Graham and Barbara relax into their roles, leaving Rachel Lonergan feeling guilty about having labelled them as game players.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor have an alternative approach to their business at The Old Mill House BandB in Bridport, Dorset. Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch think it will be a wigwam, while Rachel and Dan Lonergan are expecting a commune.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of this week of new episodes is to The Brighton Inn, where BandB newbies Rachel and Dan Lonergan want to offer a luxury, boutique experience for their guests. After getting negative feedback on the last visit, Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor go on the offensive and give their room a thorough going-over.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition and the BandBers meet for one last time to confront their rivals. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Kerry and Stewart find a little oasis on the Canary island of Lanzarote, that all their family can escape to. Leah has five fantastic properties to show them for their budget of £120,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul and Tracy are looking for their dream holiday home near Lake Vinuela in southern Spain, for Tracys 50th birthday. Danni Menzies tries to find the perfect present for their £150k.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: The story of one mans heroic attempt to fulfil his childhood dream. Having fallen in love with the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle as a child, and now he aims to bring back it back from the ruins and restore it to its former glory.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Ever since she was a teenager, artist Indi Waterstone has dreamed of building a house inspired by nature. Now, along with partner Rebecca, shes going to do just that, in one of the most remote and beautiful landscapes in Britain - the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On Merseyside, the first to host is fun-loving oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood, who has high hopes for her down-to-earth menu and her warm welcoming style of hosting.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The action on Merseyside continues with 24-year-old pastoral care manager Steven Booth the host. Steven is a mega Tina Turner fan and each of his courses is named after her songs.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Third to host on Merseyside is glamorous PR lady Jade Chantelle, who plans a casino-themed evening and is convinced her guests will be blown away by her high class menu, lashings of champagne and a game of roulette.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Anna Senior is fourth to host on Merseyside. Anna plans a fairytale-themed evening, featuring a Mad Hatters soup and a sandwich, a special pork dish cooked three ways, and baked cheesecake.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: As the week in and around Merseyside draws to a close, its the turn of 36-year-old Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers to host. Can Jamie and Jade put their differences from last night aside?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins as BandB rookies Steve and Elise host in Pembrokeshire. Theyre hoping no one will notice their inexperience after a trip to a brewery.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Anjana and David host at their unique off-grid accommodation in Powys. Alas, the outdoor toilets mean some of the BandBers are far from keen to lay down roots.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At their glampsite in Tenby, Carys and Rhys get off to a flyer thanks to the fantastic facilities - until tensions soon emerge during a trip to the zoo.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week takes the BandBers to Tanya and Garys BandB in Barmouth, where the hosts hope their incredible views will put them in sight of the win.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: After a wonderful week in Wales, the BandBers meet up once more to debate their differences and find out whos won. But will some lingering vendettas spoil the party?

programme.desc: John is rushed to AandE by helicopter after being accidently hit in the face by a golf club so hard that the club snapped. Meanwhile, Joan has come to St Georges after falling down the stairs at home and hurting her leg.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Maura, whos 85, is rushed into AandE after falling over during the night at home. She has pain in her back and medics are concerned that she cant move or feel her legs. Meanwhile, 37-year-old doctor Jim arrives in AandE after a collision with a van.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A visitor to a horse fair in Cumbria needs help after a cardiac arrest. A 12-year-old girl is treated for multiple injuries after a collision with a vehicle.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: A concerned son reaches out when his moms mysterious online boyfriend seems too shady for comfort. But Mom is hiding secrets of her own, and once Nev and Max find their man, things take a bizarre turn that leave them shocked.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Tragically, Paul passed away unexpectedly from pneumonia, triggering Michel to return to the UK to be near family and friends.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Cara hires safecracker Sam to get back her confiscated jewels, but when she insists on joining him on the job, it looks like Sam might be making the biggest mistake of his career.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A rich, lonely American comes to London to find his long lost family to share his life and fortune. Unfortunately, chance meetings with his flatmates have disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: When a pricey piece of art is stolen, its gallery owner gets his girlfriend to make copies of the painting to sell, but did the work ever leave the building in the first place?

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman discovers a nasty side to her husbands personality when he goes into hospital before their holiday. At the same time, a kind radiologist takes a shine to her.

programme.title: The Brothers Grimm
programme.desc: Nineteenth-century con artists, Jake and Will Grimm, are caught up in a fairy-tale nightmare. They travel from village to village, pretending to protect the people from enchanted creatures and carrying out exorcisms. These skills are put to the test when they come across a genuine magical curse in a haunted forest.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: Snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, young Furiosa gets caught in the crossfire of two tyrannical warlords. As the tyrants fight for dominance, Furiosa soon finds herself in a nonstop battle to make her way home.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: Adaptation of the adventures of the swinging comic-strip hero. A shy student becomes a crime fighter after a bite from a mutant spider, during a school outing, gives him extraordinary powers. As he comes to terms with his new-found abilities, he is also forced to contend with a maniac villain known as the Green Goblin.

programme.title: Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: The comic book web-slinger returns for a blockbusting sequel. Crime-fighting has cost Peter Parker his best friend and his true love Mary Jane. However, just as he quits as Spider-Man, New York comes under threat from Dr Octopus, a metal-tentacled madman. Peter has no choice but to swing back into action.

programme.title: Spider-Man 3
programme.desc: Life is sweet for Peter Parker, MJ loves him, her career is taking off and Spidey-mania is sweeping New York. However, when an alien parasite bonds to his suit, its not long before the good times and Spidey turn bad. While his ugly ego is taking centre stage, MJ is dumped from her Broadway show, Harry remembers Spider-Mans role in his fathers death, and Peter is consumed by revenge.

programme.title: Twisters
programme.desc: Competing teams of storm chasers find themselves in a fight for their lives as multiple systems converge over central Oklahoma.

programme.desc: When Trojan prince Paris woos Helen, Queen of Sparta, away from her husband Menelaus, the legendary warrior Achilles is called to lead the armies of Greece against the city fortress of Troy.

programme.title: Transformers: Revenge of the Fallen
programme.desc: Mikaela and Sam once again join forces with Optimus Prime again to save Earth from ruthless robot The Fallen.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Happy Feet Two
programme.desc: Mumbles son Erik hasnt yet found his talents, and he goes with his friends to Adelie-land which is ruled by a penguin who can fly. When Erik returns to Emperor-land and finds them trapped under big walls of ice. Will Erik be able to save his family and friends before it is too late?
